preamble: A vocalization shift where the velar nasal [ŋ] reduces towards a labio-velar approximant [w], often observed in rapid speech or specific dialectal contexts.

examples.note: Reduction and vocalization of word-final -ng in informal or fast-paced speech.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
